name: Excavate the Archives
oracle_text:
Draw three cards, then discard a card.
Salvage 3. (Look at the top three cards of your library. You may put an artifact card from among them into your hand. Put the rest on the bottom of your library in any order.)
flavor_text: "Most of what we find is junk. But junk from before the collapse is still worth a fortune."

validation_errors: Oracle text contains what looks like reminder text: "(Look at the top three cards of your library. You may put an artifact card from among them into your hand. Put the rest on the bottom of your library in any order.)"

art_prompt: A figure in tattered robes stands amid towering shelves of ancient scrolls and artifacts, hands raised as glowing blue energy swirls upward, pulling objects from the dusty archive. Scattered relics and papers drift through the air, illuminated by arcane light flooding the vast library chamber.. Stylized digital illustration, geometric composition, cool steel-blue and teal palette, screen-glow lighting, post-apocalyptic science-fantasy setting.
design_notes: Blue common sorcery at CMC 5 -- the 'complex common' slot. Draw 3 discard 1 is a classic blue common effect at 5 mana (Tidings draws 4 for 5, so draw 3 loot 1 is weaker on raw cards but adds selection). The Salvage 3 rider adds set-mechanic relevance and artifact synergy without adding meaningful complexity -- it's a clean additional look that happens after the draw/discard. Salvage at 3 is within the common range (2-3). The two effects are thematically unified: you're digging through ancient archives and pulling out useful relics. Sorcery speed keeps it from being too flexible.
